pattern: On a rotated sorted array one half around the midpoint is always sorted, so each binary-search step can decide which half could contain the target.
hints: A single rotation means the array is two sorted runs glued together; the midpoint sits inside exactly one of them.
At each step, one side of the midpoint is fully sorted; check whether the target falls inside that sorted side's range.
Binary search: if nums[lo..mid] is sorted and the target lies within it, search left, else search right; otherwise the right side is sorted, apply the symmetric test.

edge_inputs.description: target absent from a rotated array
not rotated, target at the end
rotation at the pivot, target is the minimum
target is the first element after rotation

stress.description: distinct array rotated by a random pivot with a present-or-absent target
stress.note: values are distinct (sample) as the problem requires; rotation pivot is random
